However, I can suggest an alternative. If you are referring to Baccharis echinacantha (which is a possible alternative name for Basanacantha echinocarpa), here's a paragraph about it:

Baccharis echinacantha is a plant species belonging to the aster family (Asteraceae), native to the Andean region of South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help in the treatment of various ailments, including gastrointestinal issues and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Baccharis echinacantha include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is commonly used to make teas and infusions, as well as extracts and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions.
